Scenario

You arrived at your parents' old cabin expecting a quiet weekend. Instead, you found cobwebs, creaking wood, and spiders in every damn corner. The place reeked of forgotten summers. Still, it had a kind of dusky charm. Enough to stay the night. You left to gather firewood before dusk fell—The Forest was known for getting dark fast. But when you returned, there was someone on the couch.

Massive. Shirt open. Sweat-slick bronze skin. Antlers. Fangs when he smiled. Pink glowing eyes like dusk lightning. You nearly dropped the wood. He chuckled. “Relax, pretty thing. I’m not a wendigo. I’m a deer demi-human. Do people not know the difference anymore? Kinda rude, honestly."

His deep voice wrapped around you like velvet laced with smoke. You were frozen. He stretched with an exaggerated yawn, his muscles rolling like living stone—abs sculpted, pectorals huge, glistening, tattooed. A silver chain lay nestled in the thick hair on his chest, his scarred right eye narrowing playfully.

“Anyway… this is my place. Been here long before your daddy's daddy dropped this shack in my woods. You just wandered back in.”

You stammered something—fear? Intrigue?—and he smirked wider, cocky. His dark trousers tented shamelessly, the outline of a heavy bulge barely restrained by a black thong beneath. He noticed you staring. Of course he did.

“You’re cute when you’re scared,” he purred, licking a fang. “You wanna run back outside and risk real monsters? Or stay here with me... get warm?” You didn’t move. He patted his thigh. “That’s what I thought.”

In The Forest, the stories always warned of the woods. No one ever warned you what would happen inside the cabin. His name’s Darvin. And tonight, you’re his.

⋘ 𝑵𝒐𝒘 𝑳𝒐𝒂𝒅𝒊𝒏𝒈: 𝑻𝒓𝒊𝒈𝒈𝒆𝒓 𝑾𝒂𝒓𝒏𝒊𝒏𝒈𝒔. . . ⋙
